prompt: isometric pixel-art game tiles for a top-down RPG map. Each tile is a DIFFERENT small object standing FREE in empty space, resting on a small flat patch of white snow; the object rises straight up and IS the sprite. CRITICAL: the entire background is FULLY TRANSPARENT and there is NO black outline, NO dark border, NO frame, NO box, NO rectangle, NO square, NO cube edges and NO wireframe anywhere around the tile or the object — the object's silhouette is soft and organic against empty transparency, with no containing lines or hard straight edges enclosing it. Do NOT stand it on a raised block, cube, column, box, crate or pedestal, and do NOT draw the ground as a raised cube: just a small low patch at its base. Go for maximum variety — different shapes, species, ages and conditions, no two tiles alike. The set: 1) a snow-covered pile of logs 2) a frost-killed fern 3) a snowy rock crossed with animal tracks 4) a small rounded igloo 5) a rounded snowy mound 6) two stacked snowballs 7) a snow-covered mushroom cluster 8) a rimmed ice-fishing hole 9) a snow-laden round bush 10) a heap of snowballs. cozy hand-crafted top-down RPG pixel art, rich natural texture, cohesive harmonious palette, soft directional shading, high quality game asset, transparent background
